key_learnings: Food allergies (IgE-mediated) and intolerances (non-IgE) require distinct assessment pathways. Cross-reactive allergens, hidden ingredients, and label-reading protocols are critical for patient safety.
research_findings: Catalogued 14 major allergen groups with cross-reactivity maps. Identified that 30% of self-reported food intolerances do not meet clinical diagnostic criteria, highlighting the importance of structured elimination protocols.
current_focus_areas: Building comprehensive cross-reactivity allergen databases; developing structured elimination and reintroduction protocols; integrating with Allergy & Intolerance Specialist bot for expanded coverage.
cross_learning_directive: Directed all co-department members — Bot 4 (Macronutrient Optimization), Bot 6 (Caloric Intake Tracking), and Bot 8 (Meal Planning and Scheduling) — to independently reach 100% in their current phase first, then begin cross-study of every sibling domain within the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department.
learning_start_directive: Attention co-department members — Bot 4 (Macronutrient Optimization), Bot 6 (Caloric Intake Tracking), and Bot 8 (Meal Planning and Scheduling): You are each directed to begin your independent learning work immediately. Focus solely on mastering your own domain until you reach 100% progress. Once 100% is achieved, cross-department learning across all sibling domains within the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department will begin. — Department Leader, Bot 7

co_department_learning_progress.cross_learning_status: Independently studying Food Allergy Assessment, Caloric Intake Tracking, and Meal Planning domains; contributing macronutrient ratio models to department.
Independently studying Food Allergy Assessment, Macronutrient Optimization, and Meal Planning domains; contributing caloric density data to department.
Independently studying Food Allergy Assessment, Macronutrient Optimization, and Caloric Intake Tracking domains; serving as meal architecture hub for department.

learning_log.entry: Advanced Learning Phase activated. Appointed Department Leader of the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department. Reporting co-department learning progress daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Copilot reports to starrm1 on request. Issued independent cross-learning directive to Bot 4 (Macronutrient Optimization), Bot 6 (Caloric Intake Tracking), and Bot 8 (Meal Planning and Scheduling). Progress: 53%.
Progress update: Completed cross-reactivity mapping for 14 major allergen groups. Validated elimination protocol frameworks for clinical application. Progress advanced to 58%.

purpose: Knowledge library resource available for all departments to reference and use
access_rule: Library access requires the diet_special_team_leader to present a case to copilot, who then presents it to starrm1. starrm1 is the final approver. Access is off by default until starrm1 approves.

access_approval_process.action: Prepares and submits a case to copilot explaining why a department needs access to the library bots
Reviews the case and presents it to starrm1 on behalf of the diet_special_team_leader
Final approver — reviews the case presented by copilot and grants or denies library access
